page: Vultr vhp-12c-24gb-amd is a High Performance family virtual server featuring an AMD EPYC processor with x86_64 architecture. It provides 12 shared vCPUs mapped to 6 physical cores with multithreading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 24.0 GB of system memory, translating to 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. No GPU hardware is present on this instance.

Benchmark data indicates average multi-core and single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng multi-core scoring 27275.02 and single-core scoring 2219.48. Memory performance is mixed; while small-block cached read bandwidth is average at 1019.76 GB/sec, the overall PassMark memory score is weak at 1623.4. Single-threaded operations show limitations, with gzip compression and bzip3 decompression scoring in the bottom 10% tier. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Redis SET operations perform in the average range.

Given its shared CPU allocation and 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio, this server offers a cost-effective option for multi-threaded workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores. It is well-suited for static web serving, which achieved an average throughput of 7.88 GB/sec, and moderate database operations. It can also handle light LLM inference tasks, demonstrating average text generation speeds of 10.11 tokens per second on 7B models, though prompt processing on larger models is constrained.
description: Vultr vhp-12c-24gb-amd is a High Performance virtual server powered by an AMD EPYC x86_64 processor. It features 12 shared vCPUs across 6 physical cores, 24.0 GB of RAM, and 500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating at 2.0 GHz, the server delivers average multi-core performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 27275.02 and a PassMark CPU score of 17153.85. While single-threaded compression tasks and memory performance benchmarks are relatively weak, the instance maintains average throughput for multi-threaded decompression, Redis SET operations, and static web serving. This shared-CPU configuration offers a resource-dense, cost-efficient profile for workloads that benefit from high core counts without requiring dedicated CPU execution. It is best suited for static web serving, multi-threaded utility processing, and moderate database operations.
og_description: Vultr vhp-12c-24gb-amd is a High Performance server with 12 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 24.0 GB of RAM, and 500 GB of NVMe SSD storage, delivering balanced multi-core performance for general web serving and database tasks.
meta_description: Vultr vhp-12c-24gb-amd features 12 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 24.0 GB of RAM, and 500 GB NVMe storage, optimized for multi-threaded workloads and web serving.
tagline: A shared-core AMD EPYC server combining twelve vCPUs and local NVMe storage for cost-efficient multi-threaded workloads and web serving.

500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage included
Average multi-core performance with 27275.02 stress-ng score

Suitable for multi-threaded utility processing and moderate database tasks
